title: Molten Peruvian Potatoes Papa Rellena
description: A peppery, molten Peruvian dish featuring matcha and a rich, savory filling, perfect for a date night at home. This recipe is fodmap-friendly and can be made in under an hour.

ingredients.name: Yukon gold potatoes
ingredients.notes: Choose potatoes that are high in starch for the best results.

ingredients.name: Matcha powder
ingredients.notes: Look for ceremonial-grade matcha powder for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Gruyère cheese
ingredients.notes: You can also use other types of cheese, such as cheddar or parmesan, but Gruyère is traditional for this dish.

ingredients.name: Bacon bits
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality bacon for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Chopped scallions
ingredients.notes: Use the green and white parts of the scallions.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Use fresh garlic for the best flavor.

instructions: Scrub the potatoes clean and dry them thoroughly. Poke some holes in each potato with a fork to allow steam to escape.
Rub the potatoes with a little bit of butter and season with salt and pepper. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the potatoes for 45 minutes, or until they are tender when pierced with a fork.
While the potatoes are baking, cook the bacon in a pan until crispy. Remove the bacon from the pan and set it aside on a paper towel-lined plate.
Add the chopped scallions and garlic to the pan and cook until the scallions are softened. Remove the pan from the heat and stir in the matcha powder.

Once the potatoes are done baking, remove them from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes. Slice them in half lengthwise and scoop out most of the flesh, leaving a thin layer to hold the potato together.
Mix the scooped-out potato flesh with the cooked bacon, eggs, and matcha scallion mixture. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Spoon the potato mixture back into the potato skins and top each one with a sprinkle of Gruyère cheese.
Bake the stuffed potatoes in the oven for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the potatoes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
